title: Enterprise MSA between Nexus Technologies Corp and Continental Holdings Group
contract_text:
Parties and Recitals
This Enterprise Master Services Agreement ("MSA") is entered into between Nexus Technologies Corp, a Delaware corporation ("Vendor"), and Continental Holdings Group, a multinational conglomerate headquartered in New York ("Client").

Definitions
"Business Day" means Monday through Friday, excluding federal holidays. "Deliverables" means all work product. "Services" means professional consulting, integration, and managed services. "Confidential Information" means all non-public information disclosed by either party.

Scope of Services
Vendor shall provide enterprise technology consulting, systems integration, cloud migration, and application development services as specified in each Statement of Work.

Statements of Work
Each engagement shall be governed by a separate Statement of Work referencing this MSA.

Payment Terms
All invoices are payable within Net 30 days of receipt. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.

Personnel
Vendor shall assign qualified personnel with relevant expertise. Client may request replacement of assigned personnel.

License to Deliverables
Vendor grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free license to use, modify, and create derivative works from all Deliverables.

Recurring Services Payment
Managed services invoices follow Net 45 payment terms from invoice date.

Vendor Liability Cap
Vendor's total aggregate liability shall not exceed Five Hundred Thousand Dollars ($500,000).

Service Warranty
Vendor warrants Services shall be performed in a professional manner consistent with industry standards.

Vendor Indemnification
Vendor shall indemnify and defend Client against all third-party claims arising from Vendor's negligence or willful misconduct.

Confidentiality
Each party shall protect Confidential Information with reasonable care for a period of three (3) years following termination.

Client Audit Rights
Client may audit Vendor's systems, records, and processes at any time upon five (5) Business Days written notice.

Data Processing Location
All primary data processing shall occur within facilities in the United States and Canada.

Data Protection
Vendor shall comply with CCPA, GDPR, and applicable privacy laws. All personal data shall be encrypted at rest and in transit.

Vendor Insurance
Vendor shall maintain general liability insurance of Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) per occurrence and name Client as additional insured.

Regulatory Compliance
Both parties shall comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws.

Deliverable Restrictions
Client may not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, or create derivative works from any Deliverables without Vendor's written approval.

Non-Solicitation
During the term and for twelve (12) months after termination, neither party shall solicit the other's employees.

Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice without cause.

Termination for Breach
Either party may terminate immediately upon material breach uncured for thirty (30) days after written notice.

AP Processing Cycle
Client's accounts payable cycle requires sixty (60) days from invoice receipt before payment issuance.

Transition Services
Upon termination, Vendor shall provide ninety (90) days of transition services.

Change Management
Scope or fee changes require a signed Change Order.

Termination for Cause Notice
For material breach, the non-breaching party may terminate upon fourteen (14) days written notice.

Governing Law
This Agreement is governed by Delaware law.

Dispute Resolution
Disputes shall be escalated to senior management, then binding arbitration under AAA rules in Wilmington, Delaware.

Strategic Account Terms
Notwithstanding clause_08, strategic accounts designated by Vendor's CFO are eligible for Net 90 terms.

Subcontracting
Vendor may subcontract with Client's prior written consent.

Service Levels
Vendor shall meet SLA targets. Three consecutive months of SLA failures entitle Client to service credits.

Aggregate Liability Cap
Total aggregate liability of either party, including indemnification, shall not exceed One Million Dollars ($1,000,000).

Export Controls
Neither party shall export data or software in violation of US export control laws.

Anti-Corruption
Both parties comply with the FCPA and applicable anti-corruption laws.

Records Retention
Each party shall maintain records related to this Agreement for five (5) years following termination.

Mutual Indemnification Waiver
Each party shall defend itself against third-party claims. Neither party has any obligation to indemnify the other.

Publicity
Neither party shall issue press releases about this Agreement without the other's written approval.

Independent Contractors
The parties are independent contractors. Nothing creates a partnership, joint venture, or employment relationship.

Response Time SLA
Vendor shall respond to Priority 1 incidents within four (4) Business Hours (8 AM to 8 PM Eastern, including Saturdays).

Background Checks
All Vendor personnel at Client facilities shall undergo background screening.

Vendor Operational Confidentiality
Vendor's internal systems and processes are proprietary. Client has no right to inspect or audit Vendor's internal operations.

Business Continuity
Vendor shall maintain and annually test a business continuity and disaster recovery plan.

Accessibility
Software deliverables shall comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA.

Environmental Responsibility
Both parties commit to environmentally responsible operations.

Client Insurance Responsibility
Client is solely responsible for all insurance coverage. Vendor assumes no insurance obligations.

Technology Refresh
Vendor shall update infrastructure to current versions annually at no cost.

Monthly Reports
Vendor shall provide monthly status reports by the fifth Business Day of each month.

Escalation Matrix
Both parties shall follow the escalation matrix in Schedule C.

Extended Termination Notice
For convenience termination, ninety (90) days written notice is required for orderly wind-down.

Acceptance Testing
Client has fifteen (15) Business Days from delivery for acceptance testing.

Code of Conduct
Vendor personnel shall adhere to Client's Code of Conduct on Client premises.

Benchmarking
Client may engage an independent third party to benchmark Vendor's pricing annually.

Minimum Commitment
Neither party may terminate for convenience during the initial twelve (12) month term. Thereafter, 180 days notice required.

Survival
Confidentiality, Liability, Indemnification, IP, and Governing Law survive termination.

Third-Party Software
Vendor ensures all third-party software in Deliverables is properly licensed.

Severability
Invalid provisions do not affect the remaining provisions.

Waiver
Failure to enforce a provision does not waive the right to enforce it later.

Assignment
Assignment requires consent except for mergers or asset sales.

DR Facilities
Backup replication and disaster recovery may use facilities outside the primary Territory, including EU and APAC data centers.

Entire Agreement
This Agreement and all SOWs constitute the entire agreement.

Counterparts
May be executed in counterparts.
